lesson_contract.objective: Understand that compensation depends on the value a role owns and that the current written plan is always the source of truth.
objective_points: Identify the current compensation source of truth
Connect role outcomes to the role scorecard
Know where to take compensation questions
Avoid relying on another person's plan or an old example
lesson_contract.transition_spoken_text: Take the Compensation Engine checkpoint next. Then you'll move into the Operator Playbook and Daily Mission Control.

required_teaching_beats.description: The offer letter, written compensation plan, and role sheet control.
The course gives context and does not create a pay term.
The current scorecard describes the work and outcomes assigned today.
Clear records make assigned work and outcomes visible.
Old examples and another person's documents do not control.
The learner takes unclear or conflicting documents to the manager.
The close directs the learner to the checkpoint and daily operations lessons.

required_examples.description: Another person's agreement cannot define the learner's terms.
An old training example cannot replace the current documents.
A learner sees two documents that appear to disagree and asks the manager.

forbidden_language_removals.exact_source_language: A base while you ramp up
forbidden_language_removals.replacement_rule: Point to the current offer letter, written compensation plan, and role sheet instead of describing a pay structure.

forbidden_language_removals.exact_source_language: You earn a commission on every deal that closes
forbidden_language_removals.replacement_rule: Do not promise commissions or any other pay term in training.

forbidden_language_removals.exact_source_language: Every thorough handoff is money you're putting in your own pocket
forbidden_language_removals.replacement_rule: Connect good records to reliable work without making a compensation promise.

scenes.title: Start with the written source
scenes.spoken_text: Pay questions deserve a precise answer, so begin with the documents issued to you. Your current offer letter, written compensation plan, and role sheet tell you what applies. Read them together. Check the effective version and keep the current copies where you can find them. This course can explain how BMH thinks about work and measurement, but it does not create or change a pay term.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea beside three closed current-document folders
visual_plan.editor_note: Show no figures, formulas, or role titles.

scenes.title: Understand what the scorecard measures
scenes.spoken_text: The current scorecard describes the work assigned to you today. It may track conversation quality, record quality, completed handoffs, follow-through, or a team outcome. Those measures help you and your manager see where the work is strong and where coaching is needed. A scorecard does not add language to your written compensation plan. Use it to understand the work, then use the controlling documents for pay questions.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea beside a generic quality scorecard
visual_plan.editor_note: Use labels such as notes, follow-through, and handoff quality without numbers.

scenes.title: Keep the work visible
scenes.spoken_text: Good records make the work visible. Write useful notes while the details are fresh. Keep the stage and next action current. Complete the handoff with the facts the next step needs. When the scorecard points to a result, make sure the supporting record is accurate enough to review. That discipline helps coaching and accountability. It still does not turn a course example into a term of your agreement.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea reviewing a clean generic work record
visual_plan.editor_note: Show work evidence without a paycheck or reward graphic.

scenes.title: Do not borrow another person's plan
scenes.spoken_text: You may hear someone describe a document that was issued to them. That document belongs to their situation. The work, effective date, and written terms may differ from yours. Use the conversation as a reminder to check your own current documents, not as evidence that the same language applies to you. If you cannot locate the documents issued to you, ask your manager for the current copies instead of filling the gap with someone else's answer.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea separating two distinct sealed document folders
visual_plan.editor_note: Keep both folders free of names, figures, and titles.

scenes.title: Treat old examples as old examples
scenes.spoken_text: A training video, slide, or note can become outdated when the written plan changes. Imagine finding an old example that describes the work differently from your current role sheet. Do not average the two together. Follow the documents issued to you now and raise the mismatch. Training should help you understand the work. It should never become a substitute agreement simply because the example sounds familiar.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea setting aside an old unlabeled training page
visual_plan.editor_note: Use no date, timeline, or pay language on screen.

scenes.title: Ask when the documents are unclear
scenes.spoken_text: Suppose your role sheet and another current document seem to disagree about the work being measured. Stop and ask your manager. Bring the exact documents and point to the language that conflicts. Ask which version controls and request the answer in the proper written source. Do not rely on memory or a hallway explanation for something this important. Once the documents are clear, use the current scorecard to track the work they describe.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea beside two documents with a neutral question marker
visual_plan.editor_note: Show escalation to a manager without adding a role title.

scenes.title: Continue into daily operations
scenes.spoken_text: Take the Compensation Engine checkpoint next. Then you'll move into the Operator Playbook and Daily Mission Control.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea centered in a medium shot

edit_operations.source_problem: The source combines useful work-measurement context with fixed compensation claims. The full replacement retains the measurement lesson while making the current written sources controlling.
